memoryText: Theo is five, and on Saturday he went to the golf course with Grandpa to play a Very Important Game. Theo wore tiny golf shoes and carried a club almost as tall as he was. The green stretched out like a huge world, and Grandpa taught him a very serious first swing. The ball went somewhere extremely silly, into a bush, which made them both laugh. They took a break in the golf cart for snacks and secrets, then Grandpa helped Theo line up the big putt. The ball rolled, and rolled, and dropped into the hole with a satisfying plop, and they did a victory dance right there on the green. As the sun got low they packed up together. Grandpa said trophies are nice, but days like this are the real prize.

people.name: Theo
people.appearance: a 5-year-old boy with sandy blond hair, fair skin, freckles across his nose, wearing a little argyle vest, a white polo, and a green flat cap

people.name: Grandpa
people.appearance: a man in his late 60s with silver hair and a neat white moustache, fair skin, wearing a mustard-yellow cardigan and a matching flat cap

greeting: For Theo, my favourite teammate on the greatest green in the whole wide world.
